# Bitcoin Price Action & Market Sentiment — Mid-June 2026

## Executive Summary

As of June 17, 2026 (~10:19 UTC), Bitcoin is trading at approximately **$64,859**, down **~2.5% in the last 24 hours** and **~15.5% over the past 30 days**. The market is deeply bearish in sentiment, with the Crypto Fear & Greed Index registering **22 ("Extreme Fear")** — and having been in Extreme Fear territory for at least 10 consecutive days. Bitcoin remains **48.5% below its all-time high** of $126,080 set on October 6, 2025. Despite this, BTC dominance has risen to **56.1%** of the total crypto market, indicating relative outperformance versus altcoins in the downturn.

---

## 2. Recent Price Trajectory

### 30-Day Window (May 18 – June 17, 2026)
- **30-day Open (May 18):** ~$76,756
- **30-day High:** ~$77,999
- **30-day Low:** ~$59,353
- **Current (June 17):** ~$64,859
- **Net 30-day decline:** approximately -15.5%

The price fell sharply from the high-$70K range in mid-May to a local low near **$59,353** before rebounding. The recent bounce has been modest.

### 7-Day Window (June 10–17, 2026)

Bitcoin gained ~5.5% over the past 7 days, rallying from ~$61,493 on June 10 to a weekly peak of ~$66,301 on June 15, before pulling back over the last two days.

**All 10 days of available data show "Extreme Fear."** The index bottomed at **8** on June 8 and has risen slightly to **22** as of June 17, coinciding with the modest price bounce over the week. However, 22 remains firmly in Extreme Fear territory (0–24 range).

The sharp -25.79% drop in 24h trading volume suggests declining participation and a risk-off posture across the market.

## 5. On-Chain & Network Data

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **Network Hash Rate** | ~929–956 EH/s | Blockchain.com / Mempool.space |
| **Difficulty** | 124,932,866,006,548 | Mempool.space |
| **Last Difficulty Adjustment** | **-10.09%** (block 953,568, ~June 13) | Mempool.space |
| **Daily Transactions** | 756,558 | Blockchain.com |
| **Est. Transaction Volume** | $4.82 billion | Blockchain.com |
| **BTC Mined (24h)** | 481.25 BTC | Blockchain.com |
| **Blocks Mined (24h)** | 154 | Blockchain.com |
| **Avg Block Time** | 8.95 minutes | Blockchain.com |
| **Total Blocks** | 954,081 | Blockchain.com |
| **Recommended Fee (all tiers)** | 1 sat/vB | Mempool.space |

### Key On-Chain Observations
- **The -10.09% difficulty drop** on ~June 13 is highly significant — one of the largest single adjustments in recent memory, suggesting substantial miner capitulation or hashrate migration in late May/early June.
- **Hashrate dipped to ~753 EH/s** around June 1–2 (down from a peak of ~1,130 EH/s on May 25), before recovering to ~929 EH/s currently.
- **Mempool is nearly empty** — all fee tiers at 1 sat/vB indicates minimal network congestion and reduced transaction demand.

## 6. Key Contextual Factors

- **Bear market phase:** Bitcoin is down ~39% year-over-year and ~48.5% from its ATH of $126,080 in October 2025. This places the market firmly in bear territory by historical standards.
- **Miner stress:** The large difficulty adjustment and hashrate volatility suggest miner economics are under pressure at current prices, particularly for less efficient operations.
- **Extreme Fear persistence:** 10+ consecutive days of Extreme Fear on the Fear & Greed Index is historically notable and often (though not always) associated with local bottoms or capitulation phases.
- **Volume declining:** The -25.8% drop in 24h crypto volume suggests waning participation, which can indicate either exhaustion of selling pressure or lack of buying interest.
- **BTC dominance rising:** At 56.1%, BTC dominance is elevated, typical of bear markets where capital rotates out of altcoins and into Bitcoin as a relative safe haven.
